title: Ti (III) Reagent Preparation
author: Steven W Wilhelm
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.iisccee

steps.instruction: Per 250 mL, add 4.65 g of Na 2 EDTA into distilled or Milli-Q water. undetermined, Na2EDTA
Add 3.68 g of Na 3 Citrate. undetermined, Na3 Citrate
Add 2.5 mL of 0.1 M KCl. undetermined, of 0.1 M KCl
Add 3.0 g of NaCl (s). undetermined, NaCl
Add 7.77 mL of 20% TiCl 3 while stirring after salts and chelator solution are combined in glass-stoppered bottle. undetermined, 20% TiCl3
Using 10 M NaOH, adjust pH to 8. Add NaOH dropwise while stirring to attain desired pH, let equilibrate 30-60 minutes and re-check pH. 00:30:00, equilibration

To prepare reagent, combine chelator solution and salts in a glass-stoppered bottle. This will be the step where the 20% TiCl 3 is added in while stirring. The final result should be a clear purple solution.
